lines.en_lit: therefore, we learned The majority of one in a bird and the majority two in an animal, And since there is not even an invalidation by rabbinic law, why do I to finish mitzva to complete

Abaye arranged the sequence based on tradition and in accordance with of Abba Shaul: large arrangement precedes second arrangement incense. second arrangement for incense precedes setting up two logs of wood And setting up of two logs of wood precedes the removal of ashes the inner altar. And the removal of ashes the inner altar precedes the removal of ashes from five lamps

And removal of ashes from five lamps precedes blood of the daily offering. blood of the daily offering precedes the removal of ashes from two lamps And the removal of ashes from two lamps precedes incense. incense precedes limbs limbs meal-offering meal-offering griddle-cake And the griddle-cake libations

And libations additional offerings And additional offerings vessels And vessels daily afternoon offering, as it is stated: and burn on it the fat parts of peace-offerings” on it, complete all other offerings,

The Master said: the large arrangement precedes the second arrangement for incense. From where we As it was taught itself shall go up on its bonfire upon the altar all night” this is large arrangement “And the fire of the altar shall be kept burning on it” this is second arrangement incense.
And I should reverse reasonable large arrangement precedence, as atonement it is extensive.

On the contrary, second arrangement precedence, as from it are taken inside Even so, atonement it is extensive precedence. And if you wish, say If one does not find wood for the second arrangement, wouldn’t he take from the large arrangement into

second arrangement for incense precedes the setting up of the two logs. From where we As it is written: “And the priest shall burn wood upon it in the morning, in the morning” upon it and not on the other by inference is another
But this Upon it, is needed for its own sake, Upon it, is written twice

The setting up of the two logs precedes the removal of ashes the inner altar. Although here, it is written: In the morning, in the morning, and here, it is written: “In the morning, in the morning” even so, facilitates precedence. What facilitates? two logs. But didn’t you say the two logs go to the large arrangement

Rabbi Yirmeya said: category of wood. Ravina said Since he began arrangement, he completes it Rav Ashi said: If one does not find wood for the second arrangement, won’t he take from the large arrangement into

And removal of ashes the inner altar precedes the removal of ashes from five lamps What is the reason Abaye said: I learned through tradition; I do not know the rationale And Rava said: in accordance with Reish Lakish, as Reish Lakish said: One may not forego of the mitzvot
